messages.content:
could you please drat it in more legal way
The code of ethics does not come to replace the laws, laws and professional rules applicable to the company and/or anyone
from its employees. The company's employee must be a law-abiding citizen. Compliance with these instructions is an integral part
From the code of ethics. The same goes for the company's procedures and the company's enforcement rules, the existence of which is an integral part
from compliance with the provisions of the ethical code. Every employee must recognize that any action or deed, which was done as part of his activity
in the company and which do not comply with the laws and regulations applicable to the company, may sometimes impose
Responsibility for the employee himself, for the company and for employees and other job holders

The Code of Ethics should not be construed as a replacement for any existing laws, regulations and professional standards that are applicable to the company and its employees. Each employee is expected to comply with the law and act in accordance with the ethical standards outlined in the Code of Ethics. The company's procedures and enforcement rules are integral components of the compliance with the ethical provisions. Any actions or conduct that do not comply with the applicable laws and regulations that are undertaken by the employee in the course of employment may impose legal liability on the employee, the company as well as other stakeholders.
THANK YOU
You're welcome!
please write the following in a legal way
Sure, please let me know what you would like me to write in a legal way.

Be fair and ethical in your activities with co-workers, customers, suppliers and other related parties
for company activities. Be committed to maintaining organizational discipline in all your actions and relationships with your superiors and/or
to the office bearers in the company.
 Acting with good intentions, exercising your best abilities, caring fully and placing an emphasis on avoiding showing off
Inadequacy of material matters or compromise on professionalism and integrity.
 Maintain the confidentiality of the information brought to your attention as part of your position.

To ensure that the interests of all parties involved in the company activities are protected, it is imperative that all employees engage in their activities with co-workers, customers, suppliers and other related parties in a fair and ethical manner. Each employee is expected to uphold organizational discipline in all their actions and relationships with their superiors and/or office bearers of the company.

In order to maintain professional standards, employees shall act with good intentions, execute their duties to the best of their abilities, and shall prioritize confidentiality over showing off or compromising on professionalism and integrity in material matters.

Employees are also expected to maintain the confidentiality of information brought to their attention as part of their role in the company.

please redraft it in more legal way
The Company considers itself committed to fair, ethical and proper business behavior, and its employees are committed to act in accordance with ethical and moral standards and values, both in relations between themselves and in relations with external parties, such as, customers and suppliers. The Company sees the ethical code as one of the foundations on which the culture is based its organizer and an important element in its long-term success. Accordingly, the ethical code was formulated, which is an array of rules of conduct and action aimed at ensuring that staff members and managers of the company, who will be called together below: "Employees" or "Relevant employees", will act in accordance with the criteria listed below. It is understood that the ethical code cannot, and does not even pretend to, encompass all the mandatory rules of conduct; yet the principles and values established in it can help in making the right decisions even in situations that are not regulated explicitly.

The Company acknowledges its obligation to adhere to fair, ethical, and appropriate business practices, and its employees are committed to complying with ethical and moral standards and values in their dealings with both internal and external parties, such as customers and suppliers. The Company views the ethical code as a fundamental aspect of its organizational culture and an integral part of its long-term prosperity. Consequently, the ethical code was formulated as a set of guidelines to ensure that employees and managers of the company, whom we will refer to as "Employees" or "Relevant employees," act in accordance with the principles of conduct specified therein.

It is important to recognize that the ethical code is not intended to be a comprehensive set of mandatory rules of conduct. However, the values and principles espoused within it can be useful in guiding sound decision-making, even in situations where explicit regulations do not exist.

In the performance of his duties, each employee must accept the approval of each of his superiors.
In the performance of his duties, an employee must comply with every established procedure and every instruction given to him by law and every instruction given by
Whoever is authorized to do so or it arises from his defined position, whether the position is permanent or temporary.
An employee must fulfill his work and the duties assigned to him without delay, faithfully, diligently and to the best of his ability

his ability, and he owes his work with discipline and punctuality.
An employee must be careful to maintain peace, order and cleanliness in his workplace, avoid any disruption at work
and dealing with matters unrelated to the performance of his work. The behavior and appearance of an employee, both during his work and in between
Not during his work, should be appropriate to his duties and status as an employee.
An employee must maintain punctuality in showing up to work, and will not leave his workplace before the end of the hour set for finishing
the work, unless he received permission from his supervisor.
An employee may leave his place of work within his working hours only for the purpose of performing the work, only if he fulfilled another
The instructions and arrangements established in connection therewith.
An employee must maintain safety and correctness and be careful to fulfill all the instructions related to this.
An employee will not persuade, entice or assist another employee to commit a disciplinary offense.
​

In the course of their employment, each employee is required to obtain the consent of each of their superiors. An employee must adhere to all established procedures and follow each instruction given to them by law or authorized personnel, or commensurate with their position, regardless of the duration of their post.

Each employee must engage in their work and assigned duties promptly, faithfully, and with diligence and care, to the best of their abilities.Each employee must demonstrate discipline and punctuality and ensure that a peaceful, orderly, and clean environment is maintained within the workplace. Disruption of any kind and dealing with issues unrelated to the performance of one's tasks should be avoided. The appearance and conduct of the employee, whether in the workplace or elsewhere, must be appropriate to their role and function as an employee.

An employee should strictly adhere to the designated working hours, remaining present until the conclusion of the work assigned unless prior authorization has been obtained from their supervisor. An employee's work-related absence during working hours is permitted only if it adheres to established instructions and arrangements.

Each employee must prioritize safety and accuracy and adhere to all instructions related to these principles. It is forbidden for an employee to incite or assist another employee in committing a disciplinary offense.

The company's employees will act in good faith, act and make decisions with only the good of the company in mind. Therefore, on
The company's employee must refrain from performing any action in which there is a fear of a conflict of interest between his position in the company and his affairs
the individuals, his or his relative's, or between another position of his or his relative's.
"Relative" for this matter - spouse, brother or sister, parent, parent's parents, offspring, as well as a child's offspring, brother, sister or parent
The spouse or partner of each of these.
If there is a fear of a conflict of interest, the employee must provide any information concerning the aforementioned fear of a conflict of interest immediately
to his manager and/or the employee's direct supervisor. An employee who is supposed to make a decision from which he or his relative may opt out
are hired, the authority to make the decision should be transferred to his manager. Also, an employee must avoid tying ties
Private businesses with commercial parties associated with the company and with whom the employee maintains regular relations
His work in the company. It is clarified that the prohibition applies not only to a situation of actual conflict of interest, but to any case of concern
For conflicts of interest, i.e. situations that indicate the possibility of harming the employee's ability to perform his duties
When before his eyes only the good of society.

The employees of the Company are expected to act in good faith, with the best interests of the Company in mind, and make decisions solely for the benefit of the Company. Accordingly, employees must refrain from engaging in any activity that presents a potential conflict of interest between their position at the Company and their personal or familial affairs, or any other position they or their family members may hold. 

The term "Relative," in this context, refers to a spouse, sibling, parent, grandparent, offspring, and any extended family members related to the aforementioned parties through marriage or partnership. 

If an employee becomes aware of a potential conflict of interest, they are required to inform their manager and/or direct supervisor immediately. If the employee is tasked with making a decision that could be influenced by their personal or familial interests, the power to make that decision shall be delegated to their superior. 

Furthermore, employees must avoid engaging in any private business with commercial organizations associated with the Company if they have regular dealings with them through their work with the company. It is important to note that this prohibition extends not solely to actual conflicts of interest, but also includes circumstances that could raise concerns of conflicts of interest that may potentially harm the employee's ability to perform their duties with only the interests of the Company in their field of vision.

An employee will refrain from any action that involves taking advantage of the company's business opportunity and/or any action that may constitute competition with
the company The employee must use the information that comes to him as part of his work in the company only for the purposes for which it was transferred
to him the same information, and not for the purpose of obtaining a benefit for himself and/or any third party. in relations with third parties
associated with the company in business relationships, the employee must act while maintaining the company's good and interests
hers, and without any intention of exploiting information, connections or any other business opportunity for the employee's personal benefit. also,
The employee must refrain from taking advantage of the company's reputation or its assets, including secrets, for his personal purposes
commercials, patents, trademarks, business software, marketing plans, databases, etc.

An employee must avoid any action that involves taking advantage of the Company's business opportunities, or any conduct that may be perceived as competing with the Company in any way. 

Employees must utilize the information obtained in the course of their employment only for the purpose for which it was intended, and not for their own personal benefit or that of a third party. When dealing with third parties in business relationships associated with the Company, an employee must act in the best interests of the Company and avoid exploiting any information, connections, or other business opportunities for personal gain. 

Furthermore, an employee must not misuse the Company's reputation or assets for personal or commercial gain, including but not limited to patents, trademarks, business software, marketing plans, and databases, among others.

The company's employees must behave in a fair and decent manner in their relations with third parties, including customers, suppliers
and representatives of governmental authorities. This fairness is also required in everything related to the company's competitors. The required business information
The company's activities will be accessed by the company's employees only through kosher means and according to the law. Do not misrepresent
facts, misuse confidential information, etc.
Customers must be treated with uncompromising attention regarding the required trust, professionalism and degree of confidentiality
that these relationships are binding, and for the authorized authorities subject to the cooperation required by them. Hence on all
Employees at all levels recognize that they have a personal responsibility towards the entities and organizations whose actions may affect them
be directly affected by his work.
You must take the best measures to ensure that the documents prepared by you are given to other employees of the company
and/or to customers and/or to authorities and/or to other external bodies, are accurate, reliable and based on work
professional and thorough and there were adequate control processes before their distribution as mentioned.
It is forbidden in any way to receive from the client receiving the service, a monetary payment or any monetary equivalent either as a loan or
as a gift An employee who violates this instruction is subject to immediate dismissal

The Company's employees are expected to carry out their interactions with third parties, including customers, suppliers and representatives of governmental authorities in a fair and respectable manner. A similar level of decorum is required when interacting with the Company's competitors. The Company's employees will access business information pertaining to the Company's operations strictly through legitimate channels, conforming to the standards required by law, while maintaining confidentiality and refraining from misrepresentation.

Customers must be treated with the utmost respect, exhibiting care regarding the trust, professionalism, and degree of confidentiality required for these relationships. Employees at every level should recognize their personal responsibility towards the entities and organizations that may be directly affected by their work.

Employees must exercise utmost care in the preparation of documents that are intended to be shared amongst internal stakeholders, customers, governmental authorities, and external entities to ensure accuracy, reliability, and that they are based on thoroughly professional work. Such documents must undergo sufficient control processes before they are disseminated.

In no circumstances should an employee receive any monetary payments or equivalents from a customer. Any employee found violating this directive shall be deemed liable to immediate dismissal.

The use of the company's assets and property, both tangible assets such as vehicles, telephones or office equipment, and intangible assets such as information or intellectual property, will be done by the employees solely for operational business purposes, efficiently, frugally, and for adequate business purposes. The company's employees are required to protect the company's property and assets against theft, loss and damage and are required to take reasonable and effective precautions to prevent unauthorized use and/or
unprofessional in the company's property and its assets that are under their responsibility and/or in their use.
The use of electronic means (computers, mobile phones, etc.) in the company will be subject to security procedures
The information in the company.

The Company's assets, both tangible (such as vehicles, telephones, or office equipment) and intangible (such as information or intellectual property), shall only be utilized by employees for operational business purposes, efficiently, economically, and within reason. 

It is the responsibility of the employees to ensure the safeguarding of the Company's assets and property against theft, loss, and damage. Reasonable and effective measures must be taken to prevent any the unauthorized use or unprofessional conduct that may result in the damage of the Company's assets and property under the employee's care and/or use.

The use of electronic devices such as computers and mobile phones will be subject to the Company's security procedures concerning the information stored or processed in the company's systems.

We respect customers’ privacy and maintain confidentiality in discussing their affairs, including
business information and information regarding third parties relevant to transactions.
# We view and use banking information solely for the purpose of performing our duties at the bank. We
never look at information regarding customers other than for work purposes.
# In light of the importance of protecting banking secrecy, we understand that all retrievals of customer
information are documented and monitored.

The Company values customer privacy and confidentiality in discussing their business and related third-party information related to transactions. 

We utilize and view banking information solely for the purpose of performing our professional duties at the bank. We do not access customer information for any purpose other than work-related activities. 

Aware of the importance of maintaining banking secrecy, we are conscious that all retrievals of customer information are cataloged, documented, and monitored.

please write the following in a legal way 
All information found in the company is confidential information unless otherwise stated. The company's employees are obligated to maintain the confidentiality of the information transmitted to them as part of their work in the company, including information concerning customers, suppliers, other employees, etc., that reaches them from any source.
Company employees may make use of said information only for the purposes for which the information was provided to them, and only to the extent required and may only pass it on to other company employees who require this information to perform their duties in the company.
The aforementioned does not apply only in cases where the disclosure is approved by the authorized bodies, is required by virtue of the provisions of the law or in any case where it is information that is already widely known. This guideline applies to all employees of the company even after their employment with the company has ended.
The storage of the information and its removal outside the company's offices will be subject to the company's information security procedures.

All information located within the Company is classified as confidential, unless stated otherwise. The employees of the Company are bound by an obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the information shared with them as part of their official work with the Company. This includes but is not limited to information concerning clients, suppliers, and other employees.

Such information may only be used for the purpose it was exchanged for, to the extent required and may only be disclosed to other Company employees who require that same information for fulfilling their assigned duties within the Company. 

The aforementioned regulation does not apply in situations where disclosure is sanctioned by authorized authorities, is obligated by law, or when the information disclosed is already widely disseminated. These guidelines extend to all of the Company's employees, even beyond their term of employment with the Company.

The storage and removal of information outside the Company's premises is subject to the Company's 'Information Security Procedures.'

We expect our employees and managers to pass on all relevant information
For the purpose of making a decision and/or implementing a decision, with transparency and full disclosure.
We see complaints and public inquiries as an opportunity for continuous learning
and constantly to improve our activity. In this framework, we will strive to provide an adequate response
for such complaints and inquiries and to act in order to prevent their recurrence in the future.
We are aware of the fact that in the framework of doing a lot in such a variety
In a wide range of fields of activity mistakes may occur from time to time.
We encourage our employees and managers to report mistakes as well as take action
for correction and learning lessons.
​

We require our managers and employees to transmit all pertinent information transparently and with complete disclosure to reach proper decisions and/or execute them accordingly. We consider complaints and public inquiries as opportunities for continuous learning and to improve our operations. Thus, we endeavor to offer an adequate response to such complaints and inquiries, and take measures to prevent their reoccurrence in the future.

We realize that due to the extensive range of operations carried out by the Company, mistakes may occur occasionally. We encourage our managers and employees to notify us of any errors that may arise, along with taking corrective action and embracing lessons learned.
